International students requiring a residence permit to study in Sweden must obtain a valid permit before commencing the programme.

Failure to secure the required residence permit before the start date may result in the admission being withdrawn.

Programme Start Date

1 February 2027

Why You Should Apply

This doctoral studentship provides an exceptional opportunity to conduct cutting-edge research on migration, diversity, and inclusion while studying at one of Europe’s leading migration research institutions.

Benefits include:

  • Fully funded doctoral employment
  • Four years of funded PhD study
  • Monthly doctoral salary
  • Participation in an internationally renowned research centre
  • Access to large-scale survey datasets
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Academic supervision from leading migration scholars
  • Strong international research networks
  • Professional teaching and research experience
  • Career preparation for academia, public policy, NGOs, and international organizations

Apply Here

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted no later than 31 August 2026.

Candidates should ensure that all required documents are uploaded through Malmö University’s recruitment system before the deadline.

For more information, visit the official website.

Discover more global PhD scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Doctoral Student in IMER with a Focus on Survey Studies of Diversity and Inclusion at Malmö University, Sweden | Fully Funded PhD Opportunity 2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNCAC Coalition Hiring Regional Coordinators for SWANA & Latin America 2026 | Remote International Consultancy Opportunities
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Global Civil Society Coalition for the UN Convention Against Corruption (UNCAC Coalition) has officially opened applications for two major international consultancy opportunities for professionals working in anti-corruption, governance, civil society engagement, advocacy, and human rights. The organization is currently recruiting: Regional Coordinator for Southwest Asia and North Africa (SWANA/MENA) Regional Coordinator for Latin America […]

The post UNCAC Coalition Hiring Regional Coordinators for SWANA & Latin America 2026 | Remote International Consultancy Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Civil Society Coalition for the UN Convention Against Corruption (UNCAC Coalition) has officially opened applications for two major international consultancy opportunities for professionals working in anti-corruption, governance, civil society engagement, advocacy, and human rights.

The organization is currently recruiting:

  1. Regional Coordinator for Southwest Asia and North Africa (SWANA/MENA)
  2. Regional Coordinator for Latin America & the Caribbean (LAC)

These are remote, part-time consultancy roles designed for experienced professionals who are passionate about strengthening transparency, accountability, anti-corruption systems, and civil society participation across their respective regions.

The opportunities provide selected consultants with the chance to collaborate with a globally recognized civil society network that works directly on issues related to the United Nations Convention against Corruption (UNCAC), the world’s only legally binding universal anti-corruption instrument.

About the UNCAC Coalition

The UNCAC Coalition is a global network consisting of more than 400 civil society organizations committed to promoting transparency, accountability, anti-corruption reforms, access to information, financial transparency, and human rights protections worldwide.

The Coalition works to strengthen the implementation and monitoring of the United Nations Convention against Corruption by connecting civil society actors, advocacy groups, researchers, policy experts, and governance organizations from around the world.

Its work includes:

  • Advocacy and policy engagement
  • Civil society coordination
  • Monitoring UNCAC implementation
  • Organizing trainings and workshops
  • Producing research and reports
  • Promoting transparency and public participation
  • Supporting anti-corruption reforms globally

The Coalition operates internationally and collaborates with stakeholders across governments, civil society organizations, regional institutions, and international organizations.

Available Positions

1. Regional Coordinator – Southwest Asia & North Africa (SWANA/MENA)

The Coalition is seeking a consultant to coordinate outreach, communications, advocacy, and civil society engagement activities across the SWANA/MENA region.

The selected consultant will help strengthen relationships with civil society organizations working on anti-corruption and governance issues while also supporting regional advocacy initiatives connected to UNCAC implementation.

2. Regional Coordinator – Latin America & the Caribbean (LAC)

The Coalition is also recruiting a consultant for the Latin America and Caribbean region to support regional anti-corruption engagement, civil society coordination, communications, networking, and research activities.

This role focuses on expanding and strengthening the Coalition’s presence and collaboration across Latin America and the Caribbean.

Position Type and Work Structure

Both opportunities are:

  • Remote positions
  • Part-time consultancy roles
  • International opportunities
  • Long-term consultancy engagements
  • Flexible working arrangements

Estimated Weekly Workload

Selected consultants are expected to work approximately:

  • 6 to 10 hours per week

Candidates who are flexible with their schedules and available for regional coordination activities are strongly preferred.

Compensation and Benefits

The consultancy roles offer:

  • Estimated compensation of €20 per hour
  • Potential for higher compensation depending on expertise and experience
  • Flexible remote work arrangements
  • International networking opportunities
  • Opportunity to work with global anti-corruption experts and organizations
  • Participation in regional and international advocacy activities
  • Possible travel opportunities for UNCAC-related events

International Conference Participation

Selected consultants may also:

  • Participate in the UNCAC Conference of the States Parties
  • Receive approximately 10 days of covered travel expenses for major UNCAC events
  • Represent the Coalition at regional anti-corruption meetings, depending on funding availability

Main Responsibilities

The Regional Coordinators will support a wide range of activities focused on strengthening anti-corruption civil society engagement in their regions.

Key Responsibilities Include:

Civil Society Network Development

  • Expand and strengthen regional networks of civil society organizations
  • Identify relevant anti-corruption organizations in the region
  • Encourage organizations to join the Coalition
  • Build stronger collaboration among regional members

Regional Coordination

  • Serve as a point of contact for regional civil society organizations
  • Liaise between regional organizations and the Vienna Hub team
  • Moderate regional email discussion groups
  • Facilitate collaboration among members

Events and Meetings

  • Organize and moderate 4–5 online regional meetings annually
  • Identify speakers and discussion themes
  • Coordinate workshops and trainings
  • Support virtual advocacy events

Advocacy and Research

  • Monitor anti-corruption developments in the region
  • Conduct outreach to governments and civil society actors
  • Support transparency and accountability initiatives
  • Contribute to research and advocacy activities

Content and Communications

  • Draft briefing notes and reports
  • Produce blog posts and written submissions
  • Moderate regional discussions
  • Share updates and advocacy materials

Membership and UNCAC Engagement

  • Support civil society participation in UNCAC review processes
  • Encourage production of civil society parallel reports
  • Help administer membership applications
  • Promote transparency in UNCAC implementation reviews

Required Qualifications

SWANA/MENA Regional Coordinator

Applicants must:

  • Speak and write English and Arabic fluently
  • Have strong knowledge of anti-corruption and governance issues in the region
  • Possess at least 2 years of experience working with civil society organizations
  • Be familiar with political and governance developments in the SWANA region
  • Demonstrate strong writing, communication, and moderation skills
  • Be self-motivated, organized, diplomatic, and reliable
  • Preferably be based within the SWANA region

Latin America & Caribbean Regional Coordinator

Applicants must:

  • Speak and write English and Spanish fluently
  • Have experience with civil society organizations in Latin America and the Caribbean
  • Demonstrate strong understanding of anti-corruption and governance issues
  • Be capable of coordinating regional advocacy activities
  • Possess excellent communication and networking skills
  • Show strong organizational and moderation abilities
  • Preferably be based within the LAC region

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following:

  1. CV in English
    • Maximum two pages
    • PDF format
    • No pictures
  2. One-page Motivation Letter in English
    • PDF format
    • Mention availability
    • Mention potential conflicts of interest
  3. Writing Sample
    • Maximum two pages
    • In English
    • Ideally existing professional work
  4. AI Disclosure
    • Applicants must disclose any use of AI tools in preparing application materials

Application Process

Applicants must:

  • Complete the official application form
  • Email all required documents to:
    jobs@uncaccoalition.org

Email Subject Lines

  • “Regional Coordinator – SWANA Region”
  • “Regional Coordinator – Latin America & the Caribbean Region”

Application Deadlines

SWANA / MENA Position

Deadline:
Monday, 10 August 2026

Latin America & Caribbean Position

Deadline:
Monday, 27 July 2026 (Midnight CEST)

Starting Date

Successful candidates are expected to begin:

  • As soon as possible

Why This Opportunity Matters

These positions offer a valuable chance for professionals in governance, anti-corruption, advocacy, and civil society engagement to contribute to global transparency and accountability efforts.

The selected consultants will work directly with one of the world’s leading anti-corruption civil society coalitions while helping shape regional advocacy, strengthen networks, and support international anti-corruption reforms.

Professionals interested in governance, public policy, human rights, international development, and transparency initiatives will find these roles highly impactful and career-enhancing.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply for the SWANA Regional Coordinator Application HERE.

Interested applicants can apply for the Latin American and the Caribbean Regional Coordinator HERE.

Click HERE for more information on the UNCAC Coalition for SWANA & Latin America 2026 job opportunities.

Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here.

The post UNCAC Coalition Hiring Regional Coordinators for SWANA & Latin America 2026 | Remote International Consultancy Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
